medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient was in her normal state of health until the morning of presentation, at which point she suddenly vocalized not being able to breathe and had a syncopal episode witnessed by her great-granddaughter and great-great grandson. She was unresponsive for ~4 minutes and confused for 15 minutes thereafter. When EMS was called, BP was noted to be 60/50 and hypothermic to 35 degrees C. Upon arrival to the ED, noted improvement in BP to 110s/50s and HR in 110s; she was hypoxic to 87%, with an episode of emesis. EKG showed ventricular rate 110s, wide QRS 160s, and QTc 510. Labs notable for negative trops x1, lactate 4.0->3.2, AST 54.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension, Dyslipidemia, Calcified pleural plaques, Hyperthyroidism, Vitamin D deficiency, S/P TAH-BSO, H/O positive PPD.
medical_history.family_history: No known family history of early MI, arrhythmia, cardiomyopathies, or sudden cardiac death.

physical_examination.general_examination: T 97.8, BP 96/59, HR 100, RR 18, O2 sat 98%. Patient is sleeping in bed, very hard of hearing, no distress.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Bradycardic with irregular rhythm, S1/S2, no murmurs, gallops, or rubs. Neurological exam shows AOX2, moving all 4 extremities with purpose.
